name: Basic Pate a Choux
recipeInstructions:
1. Place liquid, butter, salt, and sugar in a medium saucepan.
2. Bring mixture to a simmer.
3. Add flour all at once and stir vigorously until it forms a ball.
4. Return to medium heat and continue stirring until the paste is dry and no longer sticks to your fingers (butters will start oozing out).
5. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Transfer the paste to a bowl or electric mixer with a paddle attachment.
7. Beat in eggs one at a time, adding each egg slowly despite increased slipperiness and messiness of the mixture until it comes together.
8. Fill pastry bag fitted with round tip (½ or 1-inch) with prepared pate.
9. Pipe out as needed; for maximum puffing use right away but it can be held for several hours.
10. Basic proportions: 1:1:1:½ cup liquid, flour, egg, butter respectively. Water yields crispier choux while milk produces tender results.
11. Expansion occurs when water turns to steam and causes the dough to burst open.
